codeReview.summary: The service-product link feature was implemented with a junction table approach. The schema, API endpoints, repository, business service, and frontend integration are all correctly implemented. The original blocking issue (missing migration) was fixed by the companion fix feature m3-1-fix-service-product-migration.

issues.description: The feature description mentioned 'Modify service schema to support inventory consumption', but instead a separate junction table was used. This is actually a better approach as it doesn't require schema modification, but the feature description was not fully accurate.

fixFeature.fixSummary: Migration file 0002_service_product.sql was created with proper schema, indexes, and unique constraints. The migration was generated using drizzle-kit and committed. However, migration was not applied to database due to missing DATABASE_URL credentials - this is noted as a non-blocking issue since the migration file is ready for application.

summary: PASS - The original feature implementation is complete with proper schema, API endpoints, frontend integration, and repository/service layers. The blocking issue (missing migration) was adequately addressed by the fix feature m3-1-fix-service-product-migration, which created the migration file. Migration application was not possible due to environment constraints (missing DATABASE_URL), but the migration file is properly committed and ready for application when database credentials are available.
